title: Carbon Capture
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) is (0) a technology designed to prevent carbon dioxide emissions from entering the atmosphere. As climate change concerns intensify, CCS is being considered as one potential tool to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.||The process works (9) by capturing carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities before it's released into the air. The captured CO2 is then compressed and transported, usually (10) through pipelines, to suitable storage sites. These sites are typically deep underground geological formations, such (11) as depleted oil and gas fields or saline aquifers, where the CO2 can be safely stored for thousands of years.||Proponents argue that CCS could/might/may (12) allow continued use of fossil fuels while reducing emissions, providing a bridge to a renewable energy future. The technology could also (13) be applied to industries like cement and steel production, which are difficult to decarbonise through other means. Some pilot projects have demonstrated that the technology works and is safe (14) when properly implemented.||However, critics raise several concerns. The technology is expensive and energy-intensive, requiring significant power (15) to operate the capture equipment. There are also questions about long-term storage safety and whether the stored CO2 might eventually leak back into the atmosphere. Environmental groups worry that investing in CCS technology (16) may delay the transition to renewable energy by making fossil fuel use seem more acceptable. Despite these debates, many climate scientists believe CCS will need to play some role in achieving global climate targets.

example.text: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) is (0) technology designed to prevent emissions.

questions.explanation: Preposición 'by' introduce el método de funcionamiento del proceso.
Preposición 'through' indica el medio de transporte: tuberías.
Expresión 'such as' introduce ejemplos de formaciones geológicas.
Voz pasiva modal: 'could be applied' expresa posibilidad.
Conjunción temporal que introduce la condición de implementación apropiada.
Expresión 'power to operate' con infinitivo de propósito.
Verbo modal 'may' expresa posibilidad de retrasar la transición.
